questions.title: Bubble Sort Trace
questions.prompt:
Perform Bubble Sort and show the array after every pass.

In Bubble Sort, adjacent elements are compared and swapped if they are in the wrong order. After each full pass, the largest remaining element moves to the end.

Your task is to return or print the array after every pass.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 1000 340" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Bubble sort trace"><rect width="1000" height="340" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.74)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="44" font-size="26" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Bubble Sort Trace</text><text x="30" y="78" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Largest value bubbles to the end after each pass.</text><g font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328"><rect x="70" y="122" width="82" height="60" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)"/><text x="111" y="162" text-anchor="middle">5</text><rect x="170" y="122" width="82" height="60" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)"/><text x="211" y="162" text-anchor="middle">1</text><rect x="270" y="122" width="82" height="60" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="311" y="162" text-anchor="middle">4</text><rect x="370" y="122" width="82" height="60" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="411" y="162" text-anchor="middle">2</text></g><path d="M 110 205 C 140 245 180 245 210 205" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><text x="140" y="268" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">swap adjacent if left > right</text><rect x="610" y="106" width="320" height="148" rx="20" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.14)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="636" y="142" font-size="20" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Pattern to notice</text><text x="636" y="180" font-size="17" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">5 moves right step by step</text><text x="636" y="212" font-size="17" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">After pass 1, largest is fixed</text></svg>
hints: Compare adjacent elements.
Swap if left element is greater than right element.
After one pass, the largest element reaches the end.
Store the array state after every pass.

questions.title: Selection Sort Trace
questions.prompt:
Perform Selection Sort and show the array after every pass.

In Selection Sort, each pass finds the minimum element in the unsorted part and swaps it with the first unsorted position.

Your task is to return or print the array after each pass.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 1000 340" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Selection sort trace"><rect width="1000" height="340" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.74)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="44" font-size="26" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Selection Sort Trace</text><text x="30" y="78" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Find the minimum in the unsorted part and place it at the front.</text><g font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328"><rect x="52" y="126" width="88" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="96" y="166" text-anchor="middle">64</text><rect x="154" y="126" width="88" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="198" y="166" text-anchor="middle">25</text><rect x="256" y="126" width="88" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.24)"/><text x="300" y="166" text-anchor="middle">12</text><rect x="358" y="126" width="88" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="402" y="166" text-anchor="middle">22</text><rect x="460" y="116" width="88" height="82" rx="16" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.36)"/><text x="504" y="166" text-anchor="middle">11</text></g><path d="M 504 215 C 410 270 180 270 96 215" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><text x="212" y="296" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">minimum 11 swaps with first position</text><rect x="650" y="112" width="300" height="132" rx="20" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.14)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="674" y="148" font-size="20" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Pattern to notice</text><text x="674" y="186" font-size="17" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Left side becomes sorted</text><text x="674" y="218" font-size="17" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">One minimum is placed each pass</text></svg>

Swap it with the current boundary position.

questions.title: Insertion Sort Trace
questions.prompt:
Perform Insertion Sort and show how the array changes after each insertion step.

In Insertion Sort, the left portion is treated as sorted. Each new element is inserted into the correct place inside the sorted left portion.

Your task is to return or print the array after each outer step.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 1000 340" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Insertion sort trace"><rect width="1000" height="340" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.74)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="44" font-size="26" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Insertion Sort Trace</text><text x="30" y="78" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">The sorted left section grows one element at a time.</text><g font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328"><rect x="66" y="126" width="82" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.22)"/><text x="107" y="166" text-anchor="middle">2</text><rect x="164" y="126" width="82" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.22)"/><text x="205" y="166" text-anchor="middle">5</text><rect x="262" y="116" width="82" height="82" rx="16" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.36)"/><text x="303" y="166" text-anchor="middle">4</text><rect x="360" y="126" width="82" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="401" y="166" text-anchor="middle">6</text><rect x="458" y="126" width="82" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="499" y="166" text-anchor="middle">1</text></g><path d="M 303 218 C 280 260 230 260 205 218" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><text x="94" y="290" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Insert 4 into the sorted left part [2, 5]</text><rect x="650" y="112" width="300" height="132" rx="20" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.14)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="674" y="148" font-size="20" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Pattern to notice</text><text x="674" y="186" font-size="17" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Sorted section grows left to right</text><text x="674" y="218" font-size="17" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Good for nearly sorted data</text></svg>
hints: Start from index 1.
Treat the left side as sorted.
Shift larger elements to the right.
Insert the current value in its correct position.
questions.starter_code:
def insertion_trace(arr):
    # TODO: return array after each insertion step
    return []

print(insertion_trace([5, 2, 4, 6, 1]))
questions.solution_code:
def insertion_trace(arr):
    arr = arr[:]
    traces = []

    for i in range(1, len(arr)):
        key = arr[i]
        j = i - 1

        while j >= 0 and arr[j] > key:
            arr[j + 1] = arr[j]
            j -= 1

        arr[j + 1] = key
        traces.append(arr[:])

    return traces

print(insertion_trace([5, 2, 4, 6, 1]))

questions.title: Identify Sorting Algorithm from Changes
questions.prompt:
Given the array changes below, identify which sorting algorithm produced them.

Start:  [5, 1, 4, 2, 8]
Pass 1: [1, 4, 2, 5, 8]
Pass 2: [1, 2, 4, 5, 8]
Pass 3: [1, 2, 4, 5, 8]

Return the algorithm name.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 1000 360" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Identify sorting algorithm from trace"><rect width="1000" height="360" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.74)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="44" font-size="26" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Identify the Sorting Algorithm</text><text x="30" y="78" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Look at what changes after every pass.</text><text x="70" y="126" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Start</text><text x="170" y="126" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#5b6570">[5, 1, 4, 2, 8]</text><text x="70" y="174" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Pass 1</text><text x="170" y="174" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#5b6570">[1, 4, 2, 5, 8]</text><text x="70" y="222" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Pass 2</text><text x="170" y="222" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#5b6570">[1, 2, 4, 5, 8]</text><rect x="650" y="112" width="300" height="150" rx="20" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.16)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="676" y="150" font-size="20" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Clue</text><text x="676" y="190" font-size="17" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Large elements move right</text><text x="676" y="222" font-size="17" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">The right side becomes fixed</text><text x="676" y="254" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Answer: Bubble Sort</text><text x="30" y="322" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Bubble Sort can be recognized because the largest unsorted value bubbles to the end.</text></svg>
hints: Check whether the largest element is moving to the end.
Check whether the smallest element is moving to the front.
Check whether the left part is gradually becoming sorted.
The given trace shows the largest remaining value moving right.
questions.starter_code:
def identify_algorithm():
    # TODO: return the algorithm name
    return ""

print(identify_algorithm())

questions.title: Compare Bubble, Selection, and Insertion Sort
questions.prompt:
Compare Bubble Sort, Selection Sort, and Insertion Sort using the same input array.

Answer these:
1. Which one usually performs many adjacent swaps?
2. Which one selects the minimum and places it at the front?
3. Which one works best for nearly sorted arrays?
4. Which one builds a sorted left section?

This question checks whether you understand algorithm behavior, not only final sorted output.

examples.output:
Bubble Sort: many adjacent swaps
Selection Sort: selects minimum each pass
Insertion Sort: best for nearly sorted arrays

hints: Bubble Sort swaps adjacent elements.
Selection Sort selects the minimum every pass.
Insertion Sort inserts into a sorted left section.
Nearly sorted arrays are usually favorable for Insertion Sort.
